title: Apologies Final Scene in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Apologies; Final Scene in EoE Post by perfected_sound » Sat Jul 14, 2007 2:56 pm Was that only there to let you see the futility of humans trying to understand one another? Why would Shinji do that, why would Asuka do THAT, and why did that happen? She touches his face? Wha..? I've read as much as I can find about this scene, as it is very popular for being highly-debatable, but still I can't see why this would happen. Every time I watch it, it confuses me more. I understand that it's a highly complex situation, but I still don't see how THOSE stand as acceptable reactions for (and from) either of them. I was just wondering what everyone else thought about it.

[]: Post by Razeil100 » Sat Jul 14, 2007 3:11 pm My opinion on "I feel sick/How disgusting" are not words spoken by Asuka, but through Asuka. I think that was kinda like a final message to the fans who were so disappointed by EoTV. Like Anno telling them what he thought about them making him basterdize his vision of Eva.

[]: Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Jul 14, 2007 3:15 pm This thread will be locked soon, but since I don't think I've given my full interpretation of it, here it is: On the outside, its harsh presentation is representative of Shinji's choice to return to reality. Compare this scene's harshness (even BEFORE anything happens) to the happy, surreal finale in the series (also interesting is the blue/red shift between them). This "harshness of reality" is reiterated again through Shinji's choking of Asuka. It makes sense that the act that started Instrumentality, is the first act done when it is finished. As stated on some official NGE thing (others will tell you what), they said that this act was done by Shinji "to prove that pain once again existed" (as it does in reality). I think that interpretation is PARTLY right, but not entirely. So far we have a harsh scene setup, that is punctuated by a harsh action, representing the harsh reality at its bleakest. So then what is Asuka's caress? It's the light in the darkness. A glimmer of hope that things will be alright. That's all Anno gives us - one simple gesture. The final line: "Kimochi Warui" breaks this act - which almost acts as one of contrition and forgiveness for Shinji with the last statement for the road: Kimochi: Feeling, emotion, mood Warui: Bad What does all of this mean? In my opinion, it's Anno's ultimate message summed up. Life is hard. It can be desperately hard. It can be so hard that at times all you want to do is curl up in a ball and cry. But no matter how bad things get, no matter how bad they look, there will always be a glimmer of "hope" that never dies as long as we're alive . That's the final scene. In a series where Anno used visuals to tell about 50% of the story, it's only appropriate that NGE ends on such a strong, complex, and multi-layered visual scene. To me it captures something so well that's so rare and ephemeral. It's not just the final scene of the series, it's Anno's final statement about his journey through life. And for those who've been there, I don't know if he ever said it as succinctly, subtly, and profoundly as there.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Jul 14, 2007 4:38 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: In my opinion, it's Anno's ultimate message summed up. Life is hard. It can be desperately hard. It can be so hard that at times all you want to do is curl up in a ball and cry. But no matter how bad things get, no matter how bad they look, there will always be a glimmer of "hope" that never dies as long as we're alive. That's the final scene. In a series where Anno used visuals to tell about 50% of the story, it's only appropriate that NGE ends on such a strong, complex, and multi-layered visual scene. To me it captures something so well that's so rare and ephemeral. It's not just the final scene of the series, it's Anno's final statement about his journey through life. And for those who've been there, I don't know if he ever said it as succinctly, subtly, and profoundly as there.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 14, 2007 5:54 pm Razeil100 wrote: My opinion on "I feel sick/How disgusting" are not words spoken by Asuka, but through Asuka.
